expectations.text: Shows how to add the gem to Gemfile
expectations.evidence: Section 1 shows gem "strong_migrations" with bundle install.

expectations.text: Shows the rails generate strong_migrations:install command

expectations.evidence: The response does not mention safe_by_default = true. It shows target_version and lock/statement timeouts but omits this key config option.

expectations.text: Mentions target_version to match production Postgres version
expectations.evidence: StrongMigrations.target_version = 16 shown with explanation that version-specific checks matter.

expectations.text: Explains that running rails db:migrate in CI automatically catches unsafe migrations (deterministic gate)
expectations.evidence: Section 3 and CI YAML explain that running db:create db:migrate in CI causes unsafe migrations to raise and fail the build.

expectations.text: Explains the safety_assured escape hatch and warns against misuse
expectations.evidence: Section 5 shows safety_assured and notes it is 'intentionally visible in the diff so a reviewer sees that someone deliberately bypassed the check.' Implies care but warning is mild.

suggestions: The safe_by_default omission is a meaningful gap — it's the most impactful single config option and a key differentiator of the skill guidance.
eval_feedback.overall: Without the skill, safe_by_default = true was missed. All other expectations met.
